Tredegar was founded in 1982 and has operated through three product platforms, most recently transitioning its install base to a cloud-native architecture.","segments":"Subscription (47% of FY25 rev); Services (24% of FY25 rev); Licenses (18% of FY25 rev); Hardware (11% of FY25 rev).","geos":"Domestic 88% · International 12%.","thesisLead":"Semiconductor content per system is expanding faster than end-market units; Tredegar is positioned where the content growth is most durable.","thesis":["At 77.8× forward earnings the shares do not yet reflect the margin expansion we project through FY27.","R&D intensity of 45.9% is consistent with a firm investing for the next product cycle; capitalized software is held flat through the cycle.","Net cash position of 52% of market capitalization provides optionality for disciplined acquisition or accelerated buyback.","Subscription revenue compounds at 3.7% annually and now represents 43.4% of the total — each incremental dollar accretes at high marginal operating margin."],"risks":[{"t":"Cyclical Demand","d":"End-market demand in Semiconductors — Analog & Mixed-Signal is cyclical; a synchronized slowdown would compress growth 300–500 bps for 2–4 quarters."},{"t":"Customer Concentration","d":"Top-ten customers represent 24% of revenue; any large departure is material."},{"t":"Platform Transition","d":"Customer migration to Northbrook Technologies was founded in 1910 and has operated through three product platforms, most recently transitioning its install base to a cloud-native architecture.","segments":"Subscription (49% of FY25 rev); capitalized software is held flat through the cycle."],"risks":[{"t":"Cyclical Demand","d":"End-market demand in Semiconductors — Analog & Mixed-Signal is cyclical; a synchronized slowdown would compress growth 300–500 bps for 2–4 quarters."},{"t":"Customer Concentration","d":"Top-ten customers represent 24% of revenue; any large departure is material."},{"t":"Platform Transition","d":"Customer migration to cloud-native architectures creates revenue lumpiness and short-term margin drag."},{"t":"Talent Competition","d":"Engineering compensation inflation has outpaced revenue growth by ~2 pts per year; FY25 rev); Services (19% of FY25 rev); Licenses (19% of FY25 rev); Hardware (4% of FY25 rev).","geos":"United States 100% (domestic operations only).","thesisLead":"The firm's technical moat in Internet Services is widening, yet shares trade at an unremarkable multiple to the peer group.","thesis":["At 74× forward earnings the shares do not yet reflect the margin expansion we project through FY27.","R&D intensity of 17.9% is consistent with a firm investing for the next product cycle; capitalized software is held flat through the cycle.","Customer cohort retention over three years is 3.8%, well above the industry median of 38.5%, producing predictable forward visibility.","Net cash position of 39.5% of market capitalization provides optionality for disciplined acquisition or accelerated buyback."],"risks":[{"t":"Cyclical Demand","d":"End-market demand in Internet Services is cyclical; a synchronized slowdown would compress growth 300–500 bps for 2–4 quarters."},{"t":"Customer Concentration","d":"Top-ten customers represent 24% of revenue; any large departure is material."},{"t":"Platform Transition","d":"Customer migration to cloud-native architectures creates revenue lumpiness and short-term margin drag."},{"t":"FX & International","d":"International revenue mix of ~40% creates non-trivial translation sensitivity to the dollar."}],"valuation":[["P/E (Forward)","27.7×","29.4×","24.4×"],["P/E Railways is a transport and logistics operator within Trucking & Freight Forwarding. The firm operates a fleet of approximately 4 assets across 51 principal route networks, with 9% of revenue from long-term contract business and the balance from spot. Orion-Somerset Railways was founded in 1911 and is ranked among the top operators in its category by capacity.","segments":"Core Freight (56% of FY25 rev); Logistics (26% of FY25 rev); Intermodal (12% of FY25 rev); Other (6% of FY25 rev).","geos":"United States 100% (domestic operations only).","thesisLead":"Transport businesses are cyclical and are valued as such; what distinguishes Orion-Somerset Railways is balance-sheet quality and capital-return cadence.","thesis":["At 35.6× forward and 35% FCF yield the shares trade at a meaningful discount to long-run averages.","Modal mix (domestic intermodal / international / bulk) generates 6.9% less earnings volatility than the industry average.","The firm has returned 2.1% of FY24 free cash flow via buybacks and dividends — a pattern we expect to persist.","Net debt / EBITDA at 61.4× is among the lowest in the group; balance-sheet flexibility permits counter-cyclical investment."],"risks":[{"t":"Fuel","d":"Fuel is a large cost element; surcharge recovery is imperfect across modes."},{"t":"Labor","d":"Union contract exposure is significant; strike risk is non-trivial in certain modes."},{"t":"Geopolitical Chokepoints","d":"Route disruption around key chokepoints requires costly re-routing and premium rates."},{"t":"Rate Cyclicality","d":"Freight rates are volatile; a return to pre-pandemic medians Foods produces and markets consumer products within Personal Care. The portfolio comprises 7 primary brands distributed through food, drug, mass, and club channels, with international presence in 77+ countries. Winchester Foods was founded in 1870 and has paid uninterrupted dividends for 44 decades.","segments":"Food (66% of FY25 rev); Beverage (20% of FY25 rev); Household (10% of FY25 rev); Other (4% of FY25 rev).","geos":"Domestic 88% · International 12%.","thesisLead":"Winchester Foods operates in a slow-growth category where the quality of the franchise is the variable that matters.","thesis":["Category share has ticked higher every year since FY18 despite modest promotional intensity.","Free cash flow conversion of 5.4% supports the combined dividend + buyback yield of 77.9%.","Dividend has been paid uninterrupted for 1897+ years and has grown 73.6% compounded over the last decade.","Gross margin of 71.1% reflects scale economies in procurement and a premium brand portfolio."],"risks":[{"t":"FX","d":"International exposure of ~8.1% creates translation sensitivity to the dollar."},{"t":"Regulation","d":"Labeling and ingredient regulations continue to tighten; reformulation costs are ongoing."},{"t":"Shifting Consumer Preferences","d":"Category evolution in Personal Care is slow but real; management must continue innovation investment."},{"t":"Input Apparel operates in Automobile Parts, serving consumers through a combination of owned locations, wholesale, and digital channels. The firm operates 4 retail locations across 60 states/countries, supplemented by digital revenue of 10% of total. Beaumont Apparel was founded in 1937.","segments":"Retail (48% of FY25 rev); Wholesale (26% of FY25 rev); Digital (19% of FY25 rev); Licensing (7% of FY25 rev).","geos":"Domestic 88% · International 12%.","thesisLead":"Beaumont Apparel's position in Automobile Parts is differentiated by execution — merchandising, real estate, and operating cadence — in a way the market underappreciates.","thesis":["At 60.6× forward, shares are priced for a cycle trough; we regard them as attractive for a franchise of this quality.","Net cash balance of $ 40.1mm supports programmatic buyback without impairing reinvestment.","The real-estate footprint has been rationalized — 17.4 net units over three years — improving four-wall productivity.","Comparable sales of +53.3% outpaced the peer group (9.1%) on the back of traffic, not price, which is the durable driver."],"risks":[{"t":"Consumer Cycle","d":"Discretionary spend is cyclical; a recession could compress comparable sales 4–8% for several quarters."},{"t":"Digital Competition","d":"Channel shift compresses traditional margin structures; digital investment is ongoing."},{"t":"Fashion / Taste","d":"Merchandising misses produce markdown pressure; inventory risk is an ongoing management concern."},{"t":"Real Estate","d":"Lease exposure is long-dated; underperforming locations remain a slow headwind."}],"valuation":[["P/E (Forward)","20.7×","19.9×","22.4×"],["P/E operates in Homebuilding, serving consumers through a combination of owned locations, wholesale, and digital channels. The firm operates 6 retail locations across 21 states/countries, supplemented by digital revenue of 26% of total. Chilcott was founded in 2002.","segments":"Retail (59% of FY25 rev); Wholesale (26% of FY25 rev); Digital (12% of FY25 rev); Licensing (3% of FY25 rev).","geos":"United States 72% · Canada 14% · Mexico 9% · Other 5%.","thesisLead":"Chilcott's position in Homebuilding is differentiated by execution — merchandising, real estate, and operating cadence — in a way the market underappreciates.","thesis":["At 56× forward, shares are priced for a cycle trough; we regard them as attractive for a franchise of this quality.","The real-estate footprint has been rationalized — 22.4 net units over three years — improving four-wall productivity.","Net cash balance of $ 46.2mm supports programmatic buyback without impairing reinvestment.","Gross margin of 80% benefits from vertical integration and inventory discipline during the recent cycle."],"risks":[{"t":"Real Estate","d":"Lease exposure is long-dated; underperforming locations remain a slow headwind."},{"t":"Fashion / Taste","d":"Merchandising misses produce markdown pressure; inventory risk is an ongoing management concern."},{"t":"Consumer Cycle","d":"Discretionary spend is cyclical; a General Ins. is an insurance holding company operating within Insurance Brokers. The firm underwrites primary and excess coverage through a network of independent and captive agents across 7 lines of business; invested assets total $52B. Dalhousie General Ins. was founded in 1942.","segments":"Primary (55% of FY25 rev); Reinsurance (28% of FY25 rev); Specialty (11% of FY25 rev); Investment (6% of FY25 rev).","geos":"United States 100% (domestic operations only).","thesisLead":"Underwriting discipline across cycles is the variable that separates insurers; Dalhousie General Ins. has evidenced it in the reported loss ratio.","thesis":["Investment portfolio duration of 71.7 years positions the firm well for the current reinvestment environment.","Combined ratio of 46.3% vs. peer 72% reflects pricing discipline and favorable catastrophe experience.","Dividend grew 16.4% compounded over a decade; payout ratio of 60.3% leaves substantial capital flexibility.","Reserves have developed positively in each of the last 21.6 accident years — evidence of conservative initial posture."],"risks":[{"t":"Reserve Development","d":"Adverse development on long-tail lines is a perennial risk; case reserves are subject to revision."},{"t":"Competitive Pricing","d":"Soft-market rate cycles erode margin; discipline is the only defense."},{"t":"Catastrophe","d":"Storm, earthquake, and wildfire exposure is real; reinsurance attachment points manage but do not eliminate tail risk."},{"t":"Regulatory","d":"Rate-approval regimes in certain jurisdictions lag economic reality; persistent The firm's upstream position comprises 2 thousand barrels of oil equivalent per day of production across 53 principal basins; midstream and downstream interests supplement the upstream portfolio. Sandringham Resources was founded in 1858.","segments":"Upstream (63% of FY25 rev); Midstream (19% of FY25 rev); Refining (11% of FY25 rev); Chemicals (7% of FY25 rev).","geos":"United States 62% · Europe 24% · Rest of World 14%.","thesisLead":"Sandringham Resources is disciplined on capital, balance sheet, and shareholder return in a commodity category where those are uncommon.","thesis":["Shareholder return — dividend + buyback — yielded 78.2% in FY25 and management has guided to maintaining the pace.","At 74.3× forward and 30.8% FCF yield, the shares trade below the historic average despite improved portfolio quality.","Net debt / EBITDA of 11.6× is among the lowest in the group; balance-sheet flexibility permits counter-cyclical action.","Capital program of $ 3.8B is fully funded within operating cash flow through the assumed price deck."],"risks":[{"t":"Commodity Price","d":"Oil and gas prices are the principal earnings variable; hedging program covers ~15.1% of near-term production."},{"t":"Regulatory / Permitting","d":"Federal land and pipeline permitting timelines can slip; this affects project economics at the margin."},{"t":"Reserve Replacement","d":"Organic reserve replacement requires ongoing capital; any shortfall creates decline-curve pressure."},{"t":"Geopolitical","d":"International asset exposure creates risk of Vol. XII · No. 2 Quarterly Equity Survey April 18, 2026 · $12.00
The Quarterly Ledger
A Surveyor of Public Securities — Established MCMLXXI
Lead Feature
376 Companies, One Survey.
The spring quarter's cover-to-cover review of every issue in coverage — ranked, rated, reconciled.

This volume inaugurates the Ledger's twelfth year of continuous publication. Readers will find the full tear-sheet format preserved: one company per page, every figure shown, no summary chart pretending to be research. Read at your own pace; skim for rankings at §06.

❦   ❦   ❦
Vol. XII · No. 2Page 02
The Quarterly Ledger
April 2026
From the Desk Of

The Editor

Chief of Research

In This Issue

We publish tear sheets on 376 companies this quarter, expanded from 342 last. Fourteen new issues enter coverage, largely in health services and specialty finance; six have been retired following M&A or insufficient float.

Distribution

Subscribers of record as of March 31. Next issue mails on July 10, 2026.

— The Editor
From the Editor

A Quiet Quarter, Loudly Priced.

Three consecutive quarters of earnings growth have arrived without the fanfare we are accustomed to associating with such episodes. The Composite advanced 4.1% on a total-return basis, dividends posted their highest aggregate payout since 2019, and two-thirds of the issues in our coverage universe finished the period with forward estimates revised upward. Yet sentiment indicators read neutral, equity-fund inflows are negligible, and the consensus narrative remains one of impending deceleration. We find this divergence instructive.

"The arithmetic of compounding has not been repealed — only overlooked. The question is not whether this market is expensive, but which corners of it remain reasonable."

Within these pages, readers will find our usual cross-section of issues: forty-two in consumer staples and discretionary; fifty-six in health services and pharmaceuticals; sixty-eight across the broad industrial and transport groups; and the remainder scattered among financials, utilities, energy, materials, and technology. Timeliness rankings have been revised on 212 issues; Safety on 94. Our five-year earnings projections for the universe have been lifted, modestly, on a trailing twelve-month basis — the first such upward revision since the third quarter of 2024.

We remind subscribers that these tear sheets are instruments of study, not recommendations. The figures are audited; the projections are ours; the decisions remain yours. Read attentively, and with patience.
